title: Track Selection & Energy Flow
description: Learn how to sequence tracks to build and release energy — the DJ skill that separates good sets from great ones.

steps.instruction: Watch: a lower-energy intro track plays at 80% volume. The crowd warms up.
Watch: a higher-energy track is cued on Deck B. The BPM is slightly higher, building momentum.
Watch: the transition happens at the phrase peak. The new track feels like a step up in energy.

phases.instruction: Think of a DJ set as a story arc. You start mellow, build energy through the middle, peak at the right moment, then bring it back down. Every track is a chapter. The transitions are sentences. Your job is to keep the story flowing.

steps.instruction: Load a medium-energy track on Deck A and play it at 80% volume. This is your starting point.
Load a second track on Deck B — ideally one with more energy, or a complementary vibe. Press play on Deck B but keep its volume at zero.
Match the BPM of Deck B to Deck A. The energy shift should come from the music, not from a tempo lurch.
Listen to the phrase structure of Deck A. Wait for the end of an 8-bar phrase — the natural handoff point.
At the phrase boundary, bring Deck B's volume up to 80% and fade Deck A down. Let the new track take over.
Fade Deck A all the way out. The new track plays solo. Notice how the energy feels — higher, lower, or the same?
